title: clappertonia polyandra
intro: Clappertonia polyandra, also known as the African gum tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Capparaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in West African folk medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Clappertonia polyandra include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Clappertonia polyandra include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or internally to harness its therapeutic properties.
